mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of instances when industrial production crosses below its trend during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate combined with stable inflation below 2.5 percent creates a favorable real yield environment that compresses equity valuation multiples, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 20-day returns when inflation exceeds 2.5%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic momentum that reduces forward earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for payroll changes against 60-day returns remains below 0.01, or positive returns dominate during payroll contraction months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive reaction to GDP prints above 2.0% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a geopolitical trade tension regime reduces manufacturing sector profitability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of industrial production declines during documented trade dispute periods.
